subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d and f Block Elements

questions.q: Which of the following transition metals is known to exhibit maximum number of oxidation states?
questions.answer: Mn in the series 3d and 4d and 5d block elements

questions.q: Explain why the lanthanide contraction is more pronounced in the actinides than in the lanthanides.
questions.answer: Lanthanide contraction is more pronounced in actinides than in lanthanides because the effective nuclear charge in actinides is less than in lanthanides. This is due to poor shielding of the nuclear charge by the 5f electrons in actinides.

questions.q: The 4f orbital is not fully occupied in the ground state of which of the following lanthanide ions?
What is the reason for the inert pair effect in the 5th and 6th period elements?
questions.answer: The inert pair effect is due to the poor shielding of the nuclear charge by the 5s electrons in the heavier members of the 5th and 6th periods, which leads to a decrease in the ionization energy of the 6s electrons.

questions.q: What is the correct order of the following elements in the periodic table with respect to the oxidation state:
Explain why the lanthanide series is not a part of the periodic table of elements?
questions.answer: The lanthanide series is not a part of the periodic table of elements because the elements in this series do not show a regular trend in their properties and exhibit a continuous series of elements rather than a group of elements.

questions.q: Which of the following elements is a lanthanide?
Why is the atomic radius of the actinides smaller than that of the corresponding lanthanides?
questions.answer: The atomic radius of the actinides is smaller than that of the corresponding lanthanides because the effective nuclear charge in actinides is less than in lanthanides, due to poor shielding of the nuclear charge by the 5f electrons.
Lanthanide contraction refers to the decrease in atomic and ionic radii of the elements in the lanthanide series as compared to the corresponding elements in the same period.

questions.q: What is the reason for the occurrence of lanthanide contraction?
questions.answer: The lanthanide contraction occurs due to the poor shielding of the nuclear charge by the 4f electrons in the lanthanide series, which leads to an increase in the effective nuclear charge and a decrease in the atomic and ionic radii of the elements in this series.
